title: Oldenlandia connata
taxonomy: Oldenlandia connata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. The genus Oldenlandia belongs to the tribe Oldenlandieae. It is a member of the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants. This classification places Oldenlandia connata within the order Gentianales. intro: Oldenlandia connata, also known as creeping lophostemon, is a type of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, including its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. The bioactive constituents of Oldenlandia connata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties.
